name: External louvers / brise-soleil
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.6 on SW/SE/S façade; heritage_protection = false
literature_source: IDAE (2011), Guía técnica de instalaciones de climatización con equipos autónomos; Henze et al. (2004), Energy and Buildings
notes: Intercepts radiation before it enters glazing — far more effective than internal shading. Not permitted in heritage protection zones where external alterations are restricted.

name: Operable external sunscreen (retractable)
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.6 on SW/SE/S/W façade; heritage_protection = false
literature_source: IDAE (2011), Guía técnica de instalaciones de climatización con equipos autónomos
notes: Retractable screen — preferred over fixed louvers when the occupant needs daylight flexibility (can be raised in winter or on overcast days). Generally accepted in heritage zones better than fixed external louvers, though still an external alteration requiring case-by-case approval. Alternative to external_shading_louvers and window_external_shutters — same shading function, different operability/cost trade-off; rank as alternates of each other rather than independent recommendations.

name: Internal roller blinds
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.6 on SW/SE/S façade (low-cost fallback, always eligible when solar gain is a problem)
literature_source: ASHRAE Fundamentals Handbook Ch.18 — internal shading SHGC reduction tables
notes: Solar radiation has already entered room as heat before the blind intercepts it — limited effectiveness. Rank last among shading options. Zero disruption, no permits required.

name: Solar control glazing replacement
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.5 on SW/SE/S/W façade; glazing era pre-2006 (construction_year != post-2006)
literature_source: CTE DB-HE 2022; Pérez-Lombard et al. (2008), Energy and Buildings
notes: Replaces existing single or basic double glazing with low-e solar control glazing. Permanent improvement requiring planning permission in some jurisdictions. Higher upfront cost than shading devices.

name: Climbing vegetation screen (green façade)
applicability_conditions: SW/SE/S façade; ground floor (floor == 0) or accessible terrace/balcony; solar_gain_score > 0.4
literature_source: Pérez et al. (2011), Green walls and their interaction with the urban microclimate, Energy and Buildings
notes: Negative lifecycle carbon due to sequestration over 20+ years. Initial embodied carbon ~10–15 kgCO₂/m². Additional evapotranspiration benefit reduces ambient microclimate temperature. Requires accessible exterior space at ground level or accessible balcony/terrace.

name: Window enlargement
applicability_conditions: cross_ventilation_direct = false AND exterior_facades >= 1; structural feasibility check required
literature_source: EN 15242; Givoni (1992), Comfort, climate analysis and building design guidelines, Energy and Buildings
notes: Only effective if a second exterior façade exists or can be created, enabling cross-ventilation. Requires structural check — flag if building is load-bearing masonry. High cost but permanent ventilation improvement. Conditional effectiveness: 1–3°C range depends on whether cross-ventilation path is achievable.

name: Interior opening / transom addition
applicability_conditions: secondary_path_possible = true
literature_source: Allard (1998), Natural Ventilation in Buildings, James and James
notes: Creates partial stack-effect ventilation path through apartment. Low disruption, no structural work, no planning permission required in most jurisdictions. Particularly effective when combined with night purge ventilation. Should always rank in shortlist when secondary_path_possible = true.

name: Stack-effect roof vent
applicability_conditions: roof_exposed = true (top floor rooms only)
literature_source: Santamouris & Asimakopoulos (1996), Passive Cooling of Buildings, James and James
notes: Cost is per unit, not per m². Drives hot air out through roof by exploiting buoyancy difference. Effective for top-floor rooms where heat accumulates near ceiling. Can be combined with roof insulation.

name: Night purge ventilation (behavioural protocol)
applicability_conditions: nocturnal_recovery_fail = true AND EPW July overnight minimum (3–5am) < 22°C
literature_source: Blondeau et al. (1997), Night ventilation for building cooling in summer, Solar Energy
notes: Zero cost, zero construction. ΔT applies to nocturnal period only — does not reduce peak daytime temperature. Critical exclusion condition: if EPW Barcelona July overnight minimum exceeds 22°C during heatwave, outdoor air is too warm to provide cooling benefit and this strategy must be excluded entirely. Intervention is a protocol specifying which windows to open, sequence, and timing.

name: External wall insulation — ETICS system
applicability_conditions: dominant_wall_U_value > 1.2 W/m²K on exterior façade; heritage_protection = false
literature_source: IDAE (2016), Guía práctica de la energía para la rehabilitación de edificios; CTE DB-HE 2022
notes: External Thermal Insulation Composite System. Not permitted in heritage protection zones — use internal_wall_insulation instead. Requires building community approval and planning permission. Permanent improvement addressing heat conduction through walls.

name: Roof insulation membrane
applicability_conditions: roof_exposed = true AND roof_U_value > 1.5 W/m²K
literature_source: IDAE (2016); Synnefa et al. (2007), Estimating the effect of using cool or green roofs on energy savings, Energy and Buildings
notes: Top-floor rooms only — addresses roof as primary daytime heat gain and nocturnal recovery failure path. Often combinable with cool_roof_coating for higher combined impact. Standard membrane system applicable to flat roofs.

name: Cool roof reflective coating
applicability_conditions: roof_exposed = true (top floor, flat roof)
literature_source: Synnefa et al. (2007); Santamouris et al. (2011), Using advanced cool materials in the urban built environment, Solar Energy
notes: Highest cost-effectiveness ratio for top-floor rooms among all envelope strategies. Raises roof solar reflectance from ~0.1 (dark) to ~0.7 (reflective). Often combinable with roof_insulation. No structural work required — applied as paint or coating system.

name: Phase-change materials (PCM) in wall assembly
applicability_conditions: nocturnal_recovery_fail = true; most effective when combined with night_purge_ventilation
literature_source: Kuznik et al. (2011), A review on phase change materials integrated in building walls, Renewable and Sustainable Energy Reviews
notes: PCM shifts timing of peak indoor temperature by 2–4 hours, it does not eliminate the peak. The LLM justification string must flag this distinction explicitly. Most effective when combined with night purge ventilation so the PCM can discharge overnight. Does not reduce peak magnitude significantly without an overnight cooling mechanism.

name: Courtyard greening
applicability_conditions: building has interior courtyard; portfolio-level intervention, not room-level
literature_source: Santamouris et al. (2001), On the impact of urban climate on the energy consumption of buildings, Solar Energy
notes: Cost is per courtyard project, not per m². Negative lifecycle carbon from vegetation sequestration. Ambient ΔT benefit applies to all rooms adjacent to courtyard. Requires building community coordination. Not applicable at individual room level — portfolio or building intervention only.

name: Shared ground-floor cooling refuge
applicability_conditions: portfolio-level intervention only; not applicable at room level
literature_source: WHO (2011), Heat and Health
notes: Not a room-level retrofit — resilience and mortality-reduction metric at building/portfolio level. Cost is highly variable (conversion of existing space). ΔT not applicable to individual rooms. Recommended for buildings where many high-risk elderly occupants are identified.

name: Street tree canopy on SW elevation
applicability_conditions: urban context intervention; requires municipality coordination; SW elevation facing street
literature_source: Bowler et al. (2010), Urban greening to cool towns and cities, Landscape and Urban Planning
notes: Municipal budget item — cost not applicable at building level. ΔT is ambient microclimate reduction benefiting facades facing the planted street. Long-term intervention (trees take 5–10 years to reach shading effectiveness). Negative lifecycle carbon from sequestration. Requires coordination with local municipality.

name: Internal wall insulation
applicability_conditions: dominant_wall_U_value > 1.2 W/m²K on exterior façade; always eligible as fallback when heritage_protection = true (where ETICS is ineligible)
literature_source: IDAE (2016); CTE DB-HE 2022
notes: More accessible for tenants than external insulation — no building permit required in most cases. Slightly reduces room area (40–60 mm wall thickness added). Cold bridge risk at wall-floor and wall-ceiling junctions requires careful detailing. Should rank above ETICS when heritage_protection = true.

name: Cool / reflective façade paint
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.5; exterior wall exposed to direct solar radiation
literature_source: Synnefa et al. (2007); Santamouris et al. (2011), Using advanced cool materials in the urban built environment, Solar Energy
notes: Highest cost-effectiveness ratio in the entire library for wall surfaces. Same albedo physics as cool_roof_coating applied to vertical surfaces. Impact is greater on darker existing facades. Heritage zone check required — colour changes may be restricted. Requires municipality or community approval in many Barcelona buildings.

name: External shutters (persianes)
applicability_conditions: solar_gain_score > 0.6 on SW/SE/S façade; shutter_box_present = true
literature_source: IDAE (2011)
notes: Extremely common in Barcelona — many apartments have existing shutter boxes with broken or unused shutters. If shutter box already exists, installation cost drops significantly (replacement mechanism only). Generally accepted in heritage zones unlike external louvers. Should rank above external_shading_louvers when shutter_box_present = true.

name: Cross-ventilation behavioural protocol
applicability_conditions: cross_ventilation_direct = true ONLY — opposite condition to interior_opening_improvement
literature_source: Givoni (1992); Allard (1998)
notes: The cross-ventilation air path already exists but the resident is not using it optimally. Intervention is a protocol: which windows to open, in which sequence, at which times of day (typically 06:00–09:00 and 22:00–06:00) to maximise airflow. Zero cost, zero disruption. Should always rank in shortlist when cross_ventilation_direct = true, regardless of other conditions.
